Narrative_Meaning: Sixes signify harmony and adaptation, but they can also indicate hard work or unexpected challenges.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Unicorn card represents either a powerful, magical engine of transformation and freedom, that is difficult to find, obtain or master, or it is something entirely imaginary. It is a dream that is chased, sometimes the dream is achieved, often it is not, but may have led you to a better or worse place than you started. Occasionally the Unicorn is nothing but a Nightmare.

Significator.Plot: The Unicorn Plot is usually described as a "Snark hunt", which is a hunt for something that may not exist (Unicorn and Boojum Hunt are also used). This might be a wild goose chase, or an investigation into a conspiracy, that might not exist. Occasionally a Unicorn plot will actually involve a powerful magical creature (although rarely in a fantasy context) or artefact. Unicorn Plots often involve a Monster, although this is sometimes a human Monster who hunts the Unicorn.
Significator.Side: When only one side is marked by the Unicorn it usually means that that side will undergo a powerful transformation, or change. Just occasionally it means that the side will be rendered powerless or paralysed by a Virtue Embodiment from another side.
Significator.Character:
The Unicorn Significator indicates a Character given to flights of fantasy (who may also be a powerful supernatural creature in their own right).

They Gain Yin when they daydream, talk to themselves, or pretend to be things they do not appear to be.

They Gain Yang when they are speak with authority or demonstrate knowledge or wisdom.

They Gain Chi when they use magic, or misdirection and deception to get what they want.
Significator.Diegetic_Character: A dreamer and visionary, often detached from the mundane world and prone to flights of fancy. They possess an ethereal quality, moving between reality and imagination with ease, and may harbor hidden depths of ancient wisdom or latent magical power.
Significator.Scene: The Unicorn Scene reveals the "Otherworld" of the story, this might be the world of Fey, a world beyond a magickal portal, or some criminal underworld, or world of the Super-Rich that is divorced from normal reality. Yarn-Tellers can play the Unicorn to reveal or portal between these worlds, or to create powerful magical Monsters and events.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's Persona specifically via their Gain, this Hook should offer a chance for them to make this Gain several times ideally.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The Unicorn's Promise is a complex Hook, that can express in many ways. It might be a dream revelation, or a situation that seems dire until someone suggests what sounds like a simple solution, but is actually a more difficult, or dangerous situation than the original, it can be a situation that seems impossible, but that there is some magical solution that is perhaps achievable.
Yarn.Gain: Dream (sometimes a new dream or goal is the best Gain, other times getting what you dreamed of is better).
Yarn.Diegetic_Gain: The discovery of a new purpose or the realization of a long-held dream, providing a renewed sense of direction and wonder.
Yarn.Fray_Description: This is a Fray that seems impossible. In fact the impossible task is possible, but you have to look at the problem in a whole new way. Just as the fierce Unicorn is entirely untameable, and a vicious killer, and yet if a virgin sits nearby and waits, the Unicorn will approach her, and fall asleep on her lap, allowing itself to be tamed.
Yarn.Snag_Description: The Fray, whatever it was, was just a distraction while the Opposed Embodiment acted to achieve their Ends. Or the PCs discover that they have been misled and have worked towards the Opposed Embodiment's goal, which makes the Fray a ploy used by the Opposed Embodiment. It is usual at this point for the Opposed Embodiment to reveal that they were actually the feeble old man all along, or whoever.

NPC.Description: The starving artist is an ancient archetype, this isn't strictly that archetype. These may be starving, and they may even create art, but they get much more pleasure from the trappings of the art world than actually doing anything as uncouth as selling art. For them to be known as an artist is enough.

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: The PCs manage to sleep, but have strong powerful dreams.
Yarn.History_Description: The Unicorn card reveals an alternate history, that may differ dramatically to the accepted past, this maybe because the alternate includes magic, færy creatures, ancient astronauts, conspiracy theories, references alternate timelines, or occurs due to reality coalescence or Yarn-Teller side-stepping.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Unicorn indicates a situation has arisen that might be blamed upon a fantastic creature, but that there is a chance that the creature itself does not exist. This may be a wild goose chase or Snark Hunt, but there is always something behind the accounts or corpses, even if it is a person, pet or wild animal. Almost always a Boojum will never be clearly identified, and the situation may end with no one really sure what exactly they drove off, away, or killed.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Unicorn card indicates that the way out of this situation is through magic, it may be a particular magic artefact or spell is required, but often almost any magic will do. In genres normally without true magic, then prestidigitation, illusion and deception will have to be used instead.

Revelations.About_Description: The information is about your own side, or own self. This might reveal your weaknesses or strengths, if you aren't already painfully aware of them.
Revelations.Info_Description: How bad will or can it get? Is everyone a traitor or just a select few? How many will die of the plague? How many swords march against you?
Revelations.Vector_Description: There is a long history of dreams being an important part of a discovery, they can reveal information that it can be difficult to obtain in other ways; it is true that dreams can present information that the mind has previously observed back to the conscious mind after it has been processed in new ways. This can be used to draw attention to something that was previously overlooked, if you throw in unconscious extra-sensory perception as well, dreams can show events that are occurring far away in real time, or can just be the usual nonsense with a hidden message. Information presented in dreams is usually symbolic in some way, rather than easily understood.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: Sometimes things are magical, they may be created by magic, have access to magic, or simply be magical in nature. The Alternative information here is about the magic that surrounds and is available to the Embodiment. Can they cast spells, have a magical sword, or are they immune to magic?
Revelations.Detail_Description: Impression details can be quite varied. Some impressions like foot-prints can be obvious, letting a hunter know which way the deer or monster went, but some can be subtle, such as the minute scratches around a picked lock. Then again not all impressions are physical, there is a reason some people try very hard to make a good first impression.

Obstacle.Description: The way is blocked either by a magical illusions, figment of the imagination, an aspect of a dream/nightmare, or a powerful Færy-creature or minor Increated.

Tarot.Normal: Target describes a Scene from their past. Allows purchase of Proficiencies, Descendants, etc.
Tarot.Crossed: Player may describe a Scene from Target's past.

Ordeal.Stage_Description: An often narrow or confined route, usually straight, that passes under something, or extremely hard work.
Ordeal.Motional_Description: Whichever dragon and rider crosses the line first, wins.
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: Surrounded by danger, the Flow and Ebbing sides have found a safe space in the battlefield, perhaps under a partially collapsed building, or behind a burned-out car. Flowing Characters can Stabilise one Wound for Free, take any Action, and take stock of the battle. Ebbing Characters may only take a Healing Action or Prepare a Healing or Defence Action.
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
A Group of Monsters (usually the same type) comparable to a PC that has at least 2 ♥ Monster Facets.
  Facet    Monster  
Enigma ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Skulker — The Skulker is a shadowy figure often pulling strings behind the scenes. This may just be an ordinary villain, but their ability to pass unnoticed should be bordering upon the unnatural. Perhaps they use psychic powers, or magic, to cloud the minds of witnesses, then again perhaps they are a hacking whiz, able to bounce phone calls through international relays with some multinational picking up the tab without realizing, or perhaps they are a Cryptid, an Alien Big Cat, Wolf man, Sasquatch or one of the Huldra, the hidden people - who maybe Fæ or the imaginary friend of one of the characters, possibly made real(ish) by an alien or an Increated.
Skulkers start with a Monster Pool of Enigma Score. Skulkers may add Enigma Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they hide, and if they hide completely for a Chi Extend Duration equal to the Enigma Boon they may add Enigma Score cards to their Monster Pool (e.g. Enigma Boon 12 grants 1 card when they hide, but if the Skulker hides until Dawn / Dusk / Noon or Midnight they may add 4 cards to the Pool).
Skulkers must Discard or Play half their Monster Pool any time they are spotted or found out. Often this will be spent on an immediate Hide action. They may add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Success Levels to any stealthy, or hidden, actions or preparations. Skulkers that are hidden may play Monster Pool cards as Preparation to another’s actions as though they were making them. E.g. The Hacker can prepare a Hack attack, that is performed by a Church Website Server, in another town. A mind-bending villain could prepare a Psych Defence in the mind of his puppet. A Sasquatch might Prepare a Flee action on the hunter they have spotted in their territory, that they will trigger with a Fear emotional effect.
Heresy ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Masker — The Monster is looks entirely like something else. Perhaps they are a Demon in the form of an innocent child, or maybe they are a shape-shifter or face-dancer, able to change their appearance as they like, then again perhaps they’re just the old Janitor in a monster costume. The Masker may add Heresy Score cards to their Monster Pool whenever they change or don their disguise (or mask) and Heresy Draw cards to the Monster Pool whenever their disguise works, or they are believed in their lies. The Monster Pool adds its Size Reduced Success Levels to any Action to do with hiding, maintaining the mask, or lying. The Masker must play at least one card from their Monster Pool any time their disguise is seen, examined, or observed as part of the action.
Jeer ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Mockery — The monster is a mockery — It might be illusionary, or Undead (usually a corpse animated by a low-level Increated), but it may be a ghost, a machine or even an animal that mocks something else. The Mockery is created with a Monster Pool of Jeer Score cards, that must be played to maintain the Mockery (when disbelieved, attacked, etc). While the Monster Pool exists the Monster can pull and play Monster Pool Size Reduced cards (initially Jeer Draw) for any action the Mockery form takes that it has no suitable Annexes for. The Mockery may add Jeer Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they are mistaken for what they mock. E.g. A Skeleton Mockery might have a "Mortality Reminder" Annex, but is given a Monster Pool of 5, so it can initially pull and play 2 cards for its mocking actions (like moving around and clawing the living). However, once attacked (or disbelieved) the Skeleton must spend at least one card from the Mockery Pool, thereafter it can only draw and Play 1 card for any Action that doesn’t involve reminding someone of their own Mortality. If the Skeleton is mistaken for a living being, then it may add 1 card to its Mockery Monster Pool.
Liberty ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Nightmare — The Monster is nightmarish, or dreamlike. Such Monsters either exist in a purely psychological realm, appearing as dreams and hallucinations, that cannot really be fought and don’t physically exist, or they are extremely dangerous and powerful Monsters that really exist and may be magical or supernatural in power. Nightmares may add Liberty Draw cards to their Monster Pool if they cause a Psych wound or appear in a dream. While they have cards in their Monster Pool they may add Liberty Score (usually rolled) to everything they do. The Nightmare may spend at least one card from their Monster Pool to do something physically impossible, they might use it to fly through the air, instantly teleport, let bullets pass through themselves, discard physical wounds, or appear in the waking world as examples. A Nightmare must discard a Monster Pool Card if they cross a threshold, barrier, or liminal space or time, or take a Psych Wound. Flower-faced Humanoids and Canines from a nightmare realm, handsome soap-sellers with an odd idea for gentlemen’s clubs, facially-burned, gardening-gloved janitors turned serial killers, six foot tall invisible rabbits, night hags, shadow beings seen when falling asleep, and any character in a dream, are all good examples of the Liberty Monster.
Nature ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Elemental — The Monster is an elemental force of nature, perhaps a literal elemental or more likely metaphorical. The Elemental will add Nature Draw cards to its Monster Pool when it encounters a natural source of its element, or when it causes collateral damage to a new location. Elementals gain +1 Success Level per Monster Pool Size Reduced and +1 Score or Pips per Monster Pool card, to any and all actions to do with their natural element. The Elemental must spend at least 1 card from its Monster Pool when soaking attacks that are not based upon its element. Salamanders, Sylphs, Gnomes, and Undines, Giants (of most types), are all example Elementals, as well as Forces-of-Nature and natural disasters such as storms as well as Tsunamis, landslides, pyroclastic flows and even blizzards (most of which might be Giants anyway).
Phoenix ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Paragon — The Monster is the embodiment of a positive force or emotion, such as Love, Forgiveness, Warmth, Satisfaction or Wonder. Paragon Monsters are usually capable of causing positive (and sometimes negative) emotions via a Psych Attack in those that see them, and while they may be positive in nature, they are not necessarily good, or altruistic in their actions. The Paragon begins with a Monster Pool of cards equal to the Phoenix Score. The Paragon can discard half of their Monster Pool to multiply any Score by their Phoenix Score (usually rolled). The Paragon’s Monster Pool adds Phoenix Draw cards if the Paragon can find a source of their chosen emotion, and adds Phoenix Score cards if it can consume, interrupt or destroy this source (stopping a Character from feeling that emotion ever again would count as destroying the source, but so would eating them, changing their mood so they no longer feel that way woul be interrupting, as they could change back later). The Paragon must spend at least one card from its Monster Pool creating a Psych Attack for all observers whenever it is observed, which may be positive or Negative as the Monster / Yarn-Teller decides. The Phoenix itself is the embodiment of Resurrection, Hope and Faith, but is quite capable of burning destruction. The Golden Goose is another example, the embodiment of charity, but it evokes profound avarice in those who would own it. The unicorn is a symbol of purity, but is dangerous in the extreme to those it considers impure. Depictions of Angels are also perfect examples of positive embodiments.
  Facet    Monster

Stress.Description: Stress and its results can make a person feel like their life is spiralling out of their control, so they try to seize control again, often by an act of rebellion. This can result in all sorts of problems as parts of the Character’s life are damaged, discarded, lost, chased away, replaced, or forgotten.
Stress.Rules: Characters who are rebelling are offered a choice. They may take a new Hitch (Consuming, Vague, Doubt, Odd, Taboo or Scars) with Banes equal to the the Character’s current Stress, or they may increase another Hitch by 1 Bane for Stress equal to the current Hitch Banes, or they may discard parts of their Character such as Proficiencies, Descendants, Allies or even Annexes which will relieve Stress equal to the Cost of the lost part in Chi

Trauma.Description: Traumas always make a deep psychological impact on the Character, and can effectively create damage deep into the brain, these scars express themselves during sleep with night-terrors, corrupted or disturbing dreams and reliving waking memories. Sleep itself can become problematic for the Character, as it is not restful at all, unless they can drink or drug themselves into dreamless sleep.
Trauma.Rules: Trauma creates Night-terrors, disturbing dreams and nightmares for the Character. The Trauma initially converts all Stress into Nightmare Zones, which a placed in the Character’s Psychosocial Dreamspace. Each Nightmare Zone may have between 1 and 5 Nightmare Points within it, these Nightmare Points are created by the Stress that was placed in them. [t13ne type="table" array="nightmareZones" /]

Age.Description: An Age of Liberty is occurring. During this age the society grants freedoms, liberties and rights to members of the society, often there is a general relaxation of rules, as police and militias are stood down, take, or are given a more liberal approach to their roles. An Age of Liberty relaxes societies mores, making society more permissive and experimental as a whole, but this does not always sompletely translate to the most conservative members of the society, who may relax a little, but not to the excesses of the more liberal. An age of Liberty has a lot in common with the ages of crime, Decadence, Chaos and Ostentation, in that it is often (although not always) followed by a collapse of some sort.
Age.Diegetic_Description: A time of liberation and expansion of rights, where the old restrictions of society are cast aside. It is an era of permissiveness and experimentation, characterized by a free press, vibrant arts, and a general sense of optimism, though the sudden shift in values often creates tension with the traditions of the past.
Age.Rules: During an Age of Liberty society will be pushed towards being more open minded, granting members of the society additional rights, privileges and responsibilities, this is usually achieved by more permissive leadership, less conservatism in society in general also helps, as does a free press, media, uncensored communications, art and culture, and a youth-engagement in politics, although there is always a conservative reaction within the Age of Liberty (which can result in the creation of new stories) that often focuses on some singular aspect of the age as a step too far.
